summary: Large Dolphin library decompilation PR that replaced many asm/dolphin/card assembly files with matching C sources and expanded Dolphin CARD/OS headers needed by those matches. The PR body says this was "more copypasta from prime," and the diff supports a source-porting/matching strategy: CARDUnlock, CARDCheck, CARDMount, CARDFormat, CARDOpen, CARDRead/Write, CARDCreate/Delete/Stat/Rename, CARDBlock/CARDDir/CARDRdwr moved from asm objects to src objects in obj_files.mk. It also added or expanded support headers for CARD metadata, OSRtc SRAM structs, OSFont, OSReset constants, OSTime conversion/macros, and ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN. Review evidence is minimal: one approval with no review comments.
what_changed: Deleted asm implementations for many Dolphin CARD modules, including CARDBlock.s, CARDCheck.s, CARDUnlock.s, CARDMount.s, CARDFormat.s, CARDOpen.s, CARDRead.s, CARDWrite.s, CARDCreate.s, CARDDelete.s, CARDDir.s, CARDRdrw.s, CARDStat.s, and CARDRename.s.

Added corresponding C implementations under src/dolphin/card, including CARDUnlock.c, CARDMount.c, CARDCheck.c, CARDBlock.c, CARDDir.c, CARDRdwr.c, CARDFormat.c, CARDOpen.c, CARDCreate.c, CARDRead.c, CARDWrite.c, CARDDelete.c, CARDStat.c, and CARDRename.c.
Updated obj_files.mk to build the new src/dolphin/card/*.c.o objects instead of the old asm/dolphin/card/*.s.o objects.

Expanded include/dolphin/card.h with CARD FAT indices, work-area and system-block constants, stat/icon/banner constants, attribute flags, CARDDirCheck, CARDID, typed CARD API prototypes, extern CARDControl __CARDBlock[2], __CARDGetDirCheck, and CARDIsValidBlockNo.
Changed CARDControl details in card.h, including making mountStep/formatStep a union and changing xferred from s32 to u32.
Added include/dolphin/os/OSRtc.h with OSSram and OSSramEx definitions and __OSLockSram/__OSLockSramEx prototypes, supporting CARD ID/format verification code.
Added include/dolphin/os/OSFont.h with OSGetFontEncode, used by CARDCheck.c to validate card encoding.
Expanded OSReset, OSTime, os.h, and types.h with constants/macros/prototypes needed by the new Dolphin matches.
Touched src/dolphin/os/OSRtc.c, OSAlarm.c, and OSReset.c with additional matched C and/or matching adjustments.
Made tiny asm call-target adjustments in asm/melee/lb/lbmthp.s and asm/melee/lb/lbunknown_004.s, visible in the excerpt as calls to OSSetPeriodicAlarm.
smart_moves: Converted whole CARD modules at once and then swapped the build list from asm to C, keeping object ordering explicit in obj_files.mk.
Used the PR-stated source-copying strategy from Prime as a high-leverage route for SDK/Dolphin code, where shared library code can often be matched by porting known equivalent source.
Strengthened card.h before or alongside C ports so the decompiled code could use typed structures and meaningful constants instead of raw offsets.

Preserved internal CARD callback structure in C: local static callbacks such as WriteCallback, EraseCallback, CreateCallbackFat, DeleteCallback, read/write callbacks, and mount callbacks correspond to old local labels and function-pointer calls.

Introduced small helper macros for layout-sensitive CARD data, especially __CARDGetDirCheck(dir) and CARDIsValidBlockNo(card, iBlock), reducing repeated pointer arithmetic while keeping the generated code close to the original.

Kept async API behavior explicit by storing callbacks in CARDControl fields such as apiCallback, eraseCallback, unlockCallback, xferCallback, and by falling back to __CARDDefaultApiCallback when needed.
decomp_lessons: For Dolphin SDK code, matching often depends as much on accurate headers as on function bodies; CARDDirCheck, CARDID, OSSram, OSSramEx, and precise CARD constants unlocked many CARD modules.
When replacing asm with C, update obj_files.mk in the same PR so the build proves the new C objects truly replace the old assembly objects.

Use typed prototypes for public and internal SDK functions once argument roles are understood; this PR changed vague void*/s32 prototypes into CARDFileInfo*, const char*, CARDDir*, u16*, and CARDCallback signatures.
Represent overlapping state fields with a union when the same CARDControl offset is used by different procedures, as with mountStep and formatStep.
Preserve unsignedness when it affects codegen and comparisons; CARDControl.xferred was changed from s32 to u32.
Checksum and block-table code benefits from named FAT indices such as CARD_FAT_CHECKSUM, CARD_FAT_CHECKSUMINV, CARD_FAT_CHECKCODE, CARD_FAT_FREEBLOCKS, CARD_FAT_LASTSLOT, and CARD_FAT_AVAIL.

CARD directory and FAT verification code uses two mirrored system blocks and chooses/copies the valid or newer block; future CARD work should reuse this pattern rather than treating the work area as a flat byte buffer.

Async CARD operations commonly update directory/FAT blocks by erasing a sector, writing 0x2000-byte system blocks, and completing through nested callbacks; matching code should preserve that control flow.
naming_conventions: Internal CARD functions use the double-underscore SDK style: __CARDGetFatBlock, __CARDUpdateFatBlock, __CARDVerify, __CARDGetDirBlock, __CARDDefaultApiCallback, __CARDSyncCallback.
Public CARD async APIs use CARD*Async names, e.g. CARDCheckAsync, CARDCheckExAsync, CARDCreateAsync, CARDDeleteAsync, CARDFormatAsync, CARDMountAsync.
CARD result constants retain SDK names such as CARD_RESULT_READY, CARD_RESULT_NOCARD, CARD_RESULT_BROKEN, CARD_RESULT_ENCODING, CARD_RESULT_INSSPACE, CARD_RESULT_BUSY, and CARD_RESULT_FATAL_ERROR.
CARD FAT layout constants use CARD_FAT_* names and zero-based index values.
CARD stat/icon/banner/speed constants use CARD_STAT_* names; file permissions use CARD_ATTR_* names.
Dolphin OS private SRAM functions and types use OSSram/OSSramEx plus __OSLockSram and __OSLockSramEx.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed entire asm files only after adding C counterparts and changing obj_files.mk from asm/dolphin/card/*.s.o to src/dolphin/card/*.c.o.
Mapped local assembly labels to static C callbacks where appropriate, such as WriteCallback and EraseCallback in CARDBlock.c/CARDDir.c.
Replaced hard-coded system block arithmetic with CARD_SYSTEM_BLOCK_SIZE and CARD_NUM_SYSTEM_BLOCK while preserving 0x2000-byte block behavior.
Used typed pointer arithmetic for the CARD work area: directory blocks at (1 + i) * CARD_SYSTEM_BLOCK_SIZE and FAT blocks at (3 + i) * CARD_SYSTEM_BLOCK_SIZE.
Preserved checksum logic through __CARDCheckSum over 16-bit data and explicit checksum/checksum-inverse fields.
Used CARDIsValidBlockNo to match repeated validity checks for block numbers in allocation, free, and verify paths.
Kept callback clearing patterns visible in C, e.g. reading a callback from CARDControl, zeroing the stored field, then invoking it.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice.
One review by ribbanya approved the PR with an empty body.
The PR merged quickly on the same day it was opened; no explicit reviewer rationale is available.
follow_up_queries: Which Prime source files correspond to these CARD and OS Dolphin matches?
Did later PRs clean up duplicated CARD_STAT_SPEED_* defines in include/dolphin/card.h?
Were any of the newly added CARD C files later renamed, reformatted, or split for closer SDK naming?
Did later reviews adjust the CARDControl union or xferred unsignedness introduced here?
Which remaining Dolphin CARD asm files, if any, were left after PR #618?
